title: Open Source AI: The 2026 Ecosystem Report
author: State of AI · April 2026

slides.title: Open Source AI: The 2026 Ecosystem Report
slides.subtitle: From Experiments to Enterprise Infrastructure
slides.body: Models, benchmarks, tools, and the economics of open AI

slides.title: The Scale of the Shift
bullets: Hugging Face hosts 700,000+ open models as of April 2026
Llama 3.3 70B: 85.7% MMLU — within 4 points of GPT-4o
llama.cpp: run a 70B model on a single consumer GPU today
Open-weight models now handle ~40% of enterprise AI inference
2026: open-source AI is production infrastructure, not a hobby

slides.title: Running AI: Local vs Cloud
left: Tools: Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, vLLM
Zero per-token cost once hardware is provisioned
Full data privacy — nothing leaves your network
Works offline, no rate limits, no vendor outages
Fine-tune and merge LoRA adapters on your own data
right: Providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, Together
Access to frontier models — GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini
Managed scale: handle traffic spikes automatically
Simpler ops — no GPU memory or infra management

slides.title: Tools Powering the Open Stack
bullets: Ollama: one-line install, 100+ models, built-in REST API
vLLM: continuous batching delivers up to 24× throughput
llama.cpp: GGUF quantization — 70B runs on one GPU
Axolotl & Unsloth: fine-tune 7B models on a gaming GPU
LangChain + Ollama: local RAG pipeline in under 30 lines

slides.title: Where Open Source Wins vs Closed
left: Offline and air-gapped environments, edge devices
Research requiring direct access to model weights
right: Tasks requiring frontier reasoning and complex chains
Teams without MLOps expertise or GPU infrastructure
Multimodal tasks: vision + audio + video input
Low-latency apps using provider-optimised global infra
Rapid prototyping before committing to own infra

slides.title: From Zero to Local LLM: 8 Lines of Python
slides.code:
import ollama

# Run Llama 3.3 70B locally — no API key, no cloud, full privacy
response = ollama.chat(
    model="llama3.3:70b",
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Explain transformer attention in 3 sentences"}
    ]
)

print(response["message"]["content"])
# ~45 tokens/sec on M2 Max · $0 per query · 100% offline
# pip install ollama && ollama pull llama3.3:70b

slides.quote: Keeping AI proprietary doesn’t make it safer — it just concentrates power. Open models that can be inspected, forked, fine-tuned, and audited by anyone are the only structural guarantee we have against AI monopoly.
slides.attribution: Yann LeCun, Chief AI Scientist, Meta — paraphrased from public statements

slides.title: Tradeoffs: What Open Source Doesn’t Give You
bullets: Open ≠ safe: weights can be fine-tuned to remove guardrails
Frontier closed models still lead on complex reasoning tasks
70B model inference requires 40+ GB VRAM — plan accordingly
You own hosting, updates, and security patches — all of them
Licence risk: Llama and Gemma restrict some commercial uses

slides.title: 2027 Outlook: What’s Coming Next
bullets: Sub-10B models matching today’s 70B models — within 2 years
Open multimodal models: vision + audio + video, all open-weight
Browser-local inference: LLMs running natively via WebGPU
Standardised open agent frameworks: tool use + memory layers
Frontier reasoning gap may persist for another 2–3 years
